2000-08-01.title: Camp Zeist Journal; The Dutch Are Unfazed by a Scottish Incursion
2000-08-01.abstract: Article describnes conversion of former NATO air base at Camp Zeist, Netherlands, into courtroom for trial under Scottish law of two Libyans accused of blowing up Pan Am jumbo jetliner over Lockerbie, Scotland, in 1989; map; photo (M)

2000-08-01.title: Peres, Defeated for President, Seems Finished With Politics
2000-08-01.abstract: Shimon Peres, three-time prime minister of Israel and one of most enduring standard-bearers for Middle East peace, is defeated by Iranian-born Moshe Katsav, relatively obscure figure, in contest in parliament to become Israel's eighth president; despite distinguished career replete with many honors including Nobel Prize, defeat will almost certainly mark him as one of Israel's great losers; he has lost four of five elections for prime minister, as well as leadership battles within Labor Party that relegated him to runner-up status, first behind late Yitzhak Rabin and current Prime Min Ehud Barak; photo (M)

2000-08-01.title: German Faults 'Silence' About Attacks on Immigrants
2000-08-01.abstract: Wave of anti-immigrant violence in Germany prompts Foreign Min Joschka Fischer to suggest that silent majority may be abetting crimes by not speaking out against them; statement is thinly veiled allusion to past Germany passivity before rise of rightist violence; recent bombing in Dusseldorf that wounded nine immigrants, and spate of other attacks discussed (M)

2000-08-01.title: For Israeli President, Soft-Spoken Sephardi
2000-08-01.abstract: Profile of Moshe Katsav, Israel's new president; photo (M)

2000-08-01.title: World Briefing
2000-08-01.abstract: ASIA    INDIA: TOP ACTOR KIDNAPPED  Rajkumar, one of southern India's best-known movie stars, left, has been kidnapped by Veerappan, one of India's most notorious criminals, the police said. The actor, who is active in politics, was abducted from his farmhouse in Gajanur village in the state of Tamil Nadu, along with his son-in-law and two other men. The police said Mr. Veerappan, who has kidnapped other people, usually for ransom, had threatened the actor. The kidnappers left an audio cassette with the family, saying a government representative should contact the gang's mediator for demands.   Barry Bearak (NYT)

2000-08-02.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-08-02.abstract: THE AMERICAS    MEXICO: PLEA TO END DRUG CERTIFICATION -- President-elect Vicente Fox will propose ending Washington's certification of Latin American countries' anti-drug efforts when he meets with President Clinton on Aug 24, said Aguilar Zinser, a foreign relations adviser to Mr. Fox. The certification program has been criticized as a symbol of American domination, and countries that refuse to take part risk losing economic benefits. Mr. Zinser argued that the United States should instead help set up a regional anti-narcotics group made up of countries fighting the drug trade.   (AP)    VENEZUELA: ELECTION UNREST -- President Hugo Chavez sent troops to quell disturbances over disputed regional election results as several opposition candidates refused to accept defeat in general elections last Sunday. Tensions were particularly high in the state of Merida, where the latest results showed a pro-Chavez candidate, Florencio Porras, edging out the incumbent, Gov. William Davila, by less than 1 percent.   (Reuters)

2000-08-02.title: Pinochet Reportedly Stripped of Immunity in Secret Court Vote
2000-08-02.abstract: Chilean Supreme Court reportedly votes in secret to strip Gen Augusto Pinochet of his senatorial immunity; move would remove last legal obstacle to full-scale criminal probe of former dictator and allow his trial on kidnapping, murder and torture charges (M)

2000-08-02.title: Bosnian Woman, Describing War Ordeal, Faints in U.S. Court
2000-08-02.abstract: Suit against Radovan Karadzic, former leader of Bosnian Serbs, begins in federal court, Manhattan, with Bosnian Muslim woman fainting on stand as she describes her captivity, rape and torture in 1992 during Balkan conflict; lawyers for Karadzic, believed hiding in Bosnia, contest plaintiffs' rights to sue in United States for offenses allegedly committed abroad; suit, filed in 1993, charges he masterminded campaign of genocide, rape and torture (M)

2000-08-02.title: Israel's Leader Faces Turmoil on All Sides
2000-08-02.abstract: Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak's political problems deepen as his right-wing opponents vow new efforts to bring down his government; Likud Party leader Ariel Sharon calls for new elections; warns his coalition will call Parliament, set to begin three-month vacation, back into session if Barak tries to 'sneak' through Palestinian peace accord; bars division of Jerusalem; Moshe Katsav, first Likud candidate ever elected president, is sworn in amid turmoil and consternation within remnants of Barak's battered coalition; photo (M)

2000-08-02.title: Port-au-Prince Journal; So That Tyrants Won't Rest, Haitians Keep a Vigil
2000-08-02.abstract: Members of Sept 30th Foundation have been holding weekly protest in Port-au-Price, Haiti, since 1997 against beatings, rapes and murders committed under former military regime; are part of national movement seeking to end tradition of impunity that has allowed those with money or political clout to escape judgment; officers that led 1991 coup are finally being brought to trial in absentia, along with soldiers and paramilitary forces accused in massacre during coup era; second trial is expected in case of six policemen accused of executing several gang members last year and of killing witnesses; map; photo (M)

2000-08-03.title: Israel´s Leader Faces Turmoil on All Side
2000-08-03.abstract: JERUSALEM, Aug. 1 — Prime Minister Ehud Barak's political problems deepened today as his right-wing opponents, sensing blood with their stunning defeat of Shimon Peres, his presidential candidate, vowed new efforts to bring down his government.

2000-08-03.title: World Briefing
2000-08-03.abstract: EUROPE    CROATIA: MISSION TO WASHINGTON  The new, democratic leaders of Croatia have been granted the official visit to Washington that the late president, Franjo Tudjman, was never allowed. President Stipe Mesic and Prime Minister Ivica Racan, opposition leaders elected this year, arrive on Monday for meetings with President Clinton and other administration officials. They have moved Croatia away from Mr. Tudjman's strident nationalism and closer to Western aims in the Balkans, but face huge fiscal problems and unemployment.    Steven Erlanger (NYT)    BRITAIN: GOOD NEWS FOR PETS  Fifteen rabies-free islands -- including Australia, Japan and Hawaii -- will be added to the ''pet passport'' program that allows cats and dogs to enter Britain without being quarantined for six months. The program got under way in February, beginning with 22 rabies-free European countries. Each passport certifies the pet has a microchip implant verifying its identity and that it has been vaccinated and tested. Other regions will be considered for inclusion in the program in the near future, including the continental United States.    (AP)

2000-08-03.title: West Hopes to Curtail Diamond Trading, but Without Money and Troops
2000-08-03.abstract: UNITED NATIONS, Aug. 1 — Diamonds have become unfashionable as never before among Western powers struggling to snuff out civil wars in Africa. Appalled by pictures of children with limbs hacked off by diamond- smuggling rebels in Sierra Leone and humiliated when those rebels kidnapped international peacekeepers in May, the United States, Britain and several other countries are making control of the diamond trade a foreign policy priority.

2000-08-03.title: U.S. and Jordan Move to Cut Trade Barriers
2000-08-03.abstract: WASHINGTON, Aug. 1 — The United States and Jordan are close to reaching an agreement to cut trade barriers, the top American negotiator said today.

2000-08-03.title: Massacres in Kashmir May Be Effort to Sabotage Peace Initiative
2000-08-03.abstract: Indian Prime Min Atal Behari Vajpayee blames Pakistan for massacres in Kashmir in which at least 93 people have been killed in six separate incidents; holds Pakistan has ordered other militant groups to step up attacks in response to bid by Hizbul Mujahedeen for cease-fire and talks; Pakistan and several of groups critical of peace effort deny any role in attacks (M)

2000-08-04.title: Russians Join U.S. to Seek New Sanctions On Taliban
2000-08-04.abstract: United States and Russia agree to work together to draft and win support for expanded United Nations sanctions against Afghanistan; cooperation aims to force ruling Taliban to end support of Islamic terrorists (M)

2000-08-04.title: South Africa Veils Crime Data, Faulting System
2000-08-04.abstract: JOHANNESBURG, Aug. 2 — South Africa's minister of safety and security has ordered an indefinite moratorium on the release of crime statistics, saying the system for collecting such information here in one of the world's most crime-plagued countries is flawed and unreliable.

2000-08-04.title: World Briefing
2000-08-04.abstract: EUROPE    CZECH REPUBLIC: JEWISH CEMETERY -- The government has been sued in the European Court of Human Rights in a dispute over how to preserve a 13th-century Jewish burial ground on the Prague site where an insurance company is building its headquarters. The suit was brought by the Committee for the Preservation of Jewish Cemeteries despite a March agreement between the government and local Jews on encasing the cemetery in concrete, and building above and below it.   Steven Erlanger (NYT)

2000-08-04.title: Yugoslavs Hold 4 Westerners in Arms Case
2000-08-04.abstract: Yugoslav Army says it has arrested two Britons and two Canadians in Montenegro, accusing them of possessing weapons and explosives, in what appears to be another effort to stimulate fears of NATO encirclement before elections next month; arrest comes on heels of detention of four Dutch citizens earlier in week and days of Serbian and Yugoslav Army statements about need to preserve unity of Yugoslavia, which is made up of Serbia and Montenegro (M)

2000-08-04.title: Resistance to E-Commerce Makes Online Book Bargains a Rarity : German Publishers Fight Web Tide
2000-08-04.abstract: Trying to maintain a fixed price for books in the Internet age may seem like the fable of King Canute vainly trying to hold back the waves.

2000-08-05.title: Utrecht Journal; An Evangelist City Built On a World Foundation
2000-08-05.abstract: Seven thousands Christian evangelists from around the world turn exhibition center in Utrecht, Netherlands, into one of the most ethnically diverse, but purposefully homogenous, encampments on earth; Billy Graham Evangelistic Association is paying for their transportation and lodging at cost of millions of dollars; nine-day event is also attended by 3,000 others who can stay at hotels; photos; map (M)

2000-08-05.title: Philippines Faces Long Guerrilla War : On Mindanao, a Victory but No Peace
2000-08-05.abstract: CAMP SARMIENTO, Philippines—  The bullet-spattered walls of the local mosque, the crumpled heaps of flimsy homes, the craters by the dirt road all testify to the ferocity of the attack a month ago on what was once the military headquarters of the Moro Islamic Liberation Front.

2000-08-05.title: Britain's Queen Mother Marks 100th Birthday
2000-08-05.abstract: Queen Elizabeth the Queen Mother celebrates her 100th birthday, waving to cheering well-wishers from Buckingham Palace balcony; photo with her children and grandchildren (M)

2000-08-05.title: Ferreting Out North Korea's Nuclear Secrets: U.S. Intelligence Experts at Odds
2000-08-05.abstract: State Dept and Pentagon are at odds over extent of North Korea's nuclear weapons program, arguing over photos or snippets of intelligence from agents; State Department says Pentagon's Defense Intelligence Agency, known for its hawkish outlook, has been painting far too black a picture, surmising on sketchy evidence that North Korea has up to 10 new installations working on a covert nuclear program; DIA says State Department is too willing to overlook reports of suspicious activity; this dispute lies behind the other debate over whether to build a new national missile defense against countries like North Korea (M)

2000-08-05.title: China Objects to Taiwan Leader's U.S. Visa
2000-08-05.abstract: China criticizes United States for giving Taiwan's president, Chen Shui-bian, a visa to stop in Los Angeles on his way to Caribbean and expresses indignation at the island's annual effort to join United Nations (S)

2000-08-06.title: Belarus Is Warned to Conduct a Fair Election
2000-08-06.abstract: Senior official of Council of Europe warns Belarus that human rights body will refuse to send observers for parliamentary elections planned in October if government of Pres Alexander G Lukashenko fails to ensure that vote is free and fair (S)

2000-08-06.title: For Former Israeli Allies in Lebanon, Refugee Life
2000-08-06.abstract: Withdrawal of Israeli troops from Lebanon spells disaster for some 2,000 officers or men of South Lebanon Army, militia force of Christians, Muslims and Druze that had helped Israel maintain its grip; militia members handled many of occupation's less appetizing tasks; with their wives and children, they dash helter-skeklter across border into Israel in cars laden with personal belongings, as towns and villages where they were once overlords are overrun by Hezbollah, militant Islamic guerrilla forces that harassed Israeli forces; photo; map (M)

2000-08-06.title: Peppery Plea For Tolerance From a Chef In Montreal
U.N. Peacekeepers Arrive to Guard Israel-Lebanon Border
2000-08-06.abstract: United Nations peacekeeping troops finally begin spreading out in force along Lebanon's border with Israel, after weeks of haggling over just where border is and 22 years after their presence was first authorized; photo of Finnish troops setting up positions in Lebanon (M)

2000-08-06.title: Former Soviet Jews Find Uneasy Peace in Germany
2000-08-06.abstract: Special report, Crossing Borders, on quiet influx into Germany of Jews from former Soviet Union; Jewish population recently surpassed 100,000 for first time since Hitler dispersed and destroyed prewar community of more than 500,000; many Germans feel re-establishment of Jewish community represents victory over Nazis and what they tried to do, but ambivalence and hostility also exist over Jewish immigration in German population whose full range of feelings over new arrivals is unknown for simple reason that they have never been consulted; awkward moment has been reached in German-Jewish relations as Jews pour at rate of 5,000 per year with support of state payments of tens of millions of dollars amid rising impatience with Holocaust-induced obligations; with recent agreement for multibillion-dollar payment to Nazi-era slave laborers and three major Jewish institutions under construction in new capital of Berlin, Germans have feeling of 'enough already' (L)

2000-08-07.title: Scientists to Announce Discovery of 10 Planets
2000-08-07.abstract: Astronomers at University of California (Berkley), MacDonald Observatory at Texas University and Geneva Observatory (Switzerland) report discovery of 10 new planets as well as tantalizing hints of another multiplanet system; Drs Debra A Fischer, Michel Mayor and William Cochran will make official announcement at International Astronomical Union meeting this week in Manchester (England) (M)

2000-08-07.title: Historic Seminary in Turkey Is Ready but Empty
2000-08-07.abstract: Holy Theological School of Chalki, one of most historic and important seminaries in Orthodox Christianity which operated for more than 1,000 years before Turkish government closed it in 1971, remains shuttered under law that gives government control over higher education in religious and military affairs; authorities insist that school must remain closed, despite appeals of Orthodox leaders and Turkey's allies, most recently Pres Clinton; while primary motive for law was to impose government authority over training Islamic clerics, it has ended up snaring theological seminary run by Greek Orthodox Church; photo; maps (M)

2000-08-07.title: Britain Fights Tide of Anti-Pedophile Attacks
2000-08-07.abstract: British government says it will urgently press for stricter laws on child molesting but resists widespread calls for public identification of released violators, who have been associated with current vigilante attacks nationwide (M)

2000-08-07.title: Egypt Says Democracy Backer Spied for U.S.
2000-08-07.abstract: Egyptian state prosecutor today expands list of accusations against Saad Eddin Ibrahim, prominent democracy advocate detained since June 30 without being formally charged, to include espionage for US; Ibrahim and supporters believe imprisonment is meant to prevent him from monitoring parliamentary elections scheduled for November; Pres Hosni Mubarak's National Democratic Party holds 97 percent of seats in People's Assembly, but elections have usually drawn charges of voter fraud; Egyptian Constitutional Court has ruled that last three parliamentary elections were flawed (M)

2000-08-07.title: Mexico City Journal; In a City Where Good Luck Meets Hope, Signs of Magic
2000-08-07.abstract: Map of Mexico City's 70,000-odd streets, with plethora of esoteric and surreal street names, is like literary masterpiece; many streets have same names, and guide reads as if mapmakers translated encyclopedia, cut work into confetti and sprinkled it over city; maps; photo (M)

2000-08-08.title: New Attacks Raise Fears About Anti-Semitism in Germany
2000-08-08.abstract: Two Jewish cemeteries are desecrated in western German state of Rhineland-Palatinate and explosive device is found outside Jewish family's home in Bavaria, contributing to climate of mounting concern in Germany over anti-Semitic and anti-immigrant violence by rightists; while attacks reflect activities of small minority, evidence that racist criminals are now better armed and organized has alarmed government of Chancellor Gerhard Schroder, which is promoting new initiative in which famous Germans will exhort nation via advertising campaign to show zero tolerance for racists; recent bombing in Dusseldorf, which wounded nine people including six Jews, noted; map (M)

2000-08-08.title: South Korea Replaces Top Economic Officials
2000-08-08.abstract: South Korea's Pres Kim Dae Jung, worried about slow pace of financial reforms and stubbornly high debts of its big businesses and banks, replaces almost half of his 19-member cabinet, including nation's top economic officials; changes are almost entirely related to performance of economy, which made remarkable recovery since severe crisis nearly three yaers ago but has lately shown signs of stress; most significant change is appointment of Jin Nyum, former planning and budget minister in charge of privatizing state-run companies, as new minister of finance and economy replacing Lee Hun Jai; photo (M)

2000-08-08.title: Moscow Journal; With Bunkers Manned, Vodka War Is in Stalemate
2000-08-08.abstract: Article on Russian government's effort to reconsolidate control over lucrative vodka industry that has set off latest round of business battles; country is riveted on struggle for control of Krystall factory near Moscow that makes Stolichnaya brand and is one of its oldest vodka-producing plants; map; photo (M)

2000-08-08.title: Indonesia's Quirky Leader Apologizes and Pledges Order
2000-08-08.abstract: Indonesia's Pres Abdurrahman Wahid apologizes in state of the union speech for falling short of expectations as country struggles to build working democracy and says he will name executive minister to bring order to his flailing administration; says he will concentrate more on foreign affairs in future and create sort of prime ministerial post to handle day-to-day domestic administration; admired for his integrity, democratic ideals and intellect, he has drawn constant criticism for disorganized and impulsive leadership style (M)

2000-08-08.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-08-08.abstract: EUROPE    YUGOSLAVIA: TERROR CHARGES FOR 4 WESTERNERS -- The Yugoslav Army says it will press terrorism charges against two Canadians and two Britons arrested in Montenegro last week. Britain has described its two citizens, Adrian Prangnell and John Yore, as policemen helping to train a new force in Kosovo. They were traveling with the Canadians, Shaun Going, who also worked in Kosovo, and his nephew Liam Hall. Like many Westerners visiting Montenegro, the four did not have Yugoslav visas. Dutch officials are also seeking access to four of their nationals arrested some three weeks ago in Serbia and accused of plotting to kidnap President Slobodan Milosevic.   (Reuters)    YUGOSLAVIA: NEW MILOSEVIC CHALLENGER -- Formalizing the breach in opposition ranks and dimming chances of voting out Mr. Milosevic, a coalition chose a new challenger, Vojislav Kostunica, left, to run against him. Most opposition parties endorsed Mr. Kostunica, a moderate nationalist. But the Serbian Renewal Movement, the single largest opposition party, chose another candidate, the Belgrade mayor, Vojislav Mihajlovic.   (AP)

2000-08-09.title: Iran's Conservative Press Court Claims New Victim
2000-08-09.abstract: TEHRAN—  Iran's conservative leadership on Tuesday closed the country's last major progressive newspaper, just days after the supreme leader, Ayatollah Sayed Ali Khamenei, declared that the progressive press threatened the national interest.

2000-08-09.title: End of a Malaysian Drama, and Very Likely of a Career
2000-08-09.abstract: Malaysian convicts Anwar Ibrahim, former deputy prime minister, of sodomy and sentences him to nine years in prison; trial called into question motives of Malaysia's leaders and credibility of courts and exposed deeply traditional nation to sordid spectacle; Anwar charges Prime Min Mahathir Mohamad orchestrated conspiracy to destroy him and scores judiciary for doing Mahathir's bidding; court also finds Anwar's brother, Sukma Darmawan, guilty of sodomy; sentences him to six years and four strokes with rattan cane; both men were convicted of engaging in homosexual relationship with family's former driver; Anwar escaped caning because of his age; plans to appeal sentence, which supporters expect to spur political unrest; there is little evidence outside courthouse of outrage that drew thousands to streets after Anwar's corruption conviction (M)

2000-08-09.title: Gore and Running Mate Hail 'Common Ideals'
2000-08-09.abstract: WASHINGTON—  Vice President Al Gore warmly introduced his running mate Tuesday in Tennessee, saying Senator Joseph Lieberman, as the first Jew on a U.S. major-party presidential ticket, offered Americans an opportunity "to tear down a mighty wall of division."

2000-08-09.title: Pinochet Ruled No Longer Immune From Prosecution
2000-08-09.abstract: Chilean Supreme Court divests Gen Augusto Pinochet of his senatorial immunity, removing legal hurdle to charging former dictator with role in kidnapping, torture and murder of thousands of people during his 17-year rule; demonstrators hail decision, Santiago; decision, which also ratifies legal doctrine that anyone who disappeared during dictatorship and is still missing is victim of ongoing kidnapping not covered by amnesty, will likely lead to prosecution of dozens of other retired military and police officers; ruling provokes veiled sniping between Pres Ricardo Lagos, Socialist, and army commander Gen Ricardo Izurieta, who has tried to coexist with civilian presidents and keep right-wing elements at bay; Izurieta makes provocative visit to Pinochet's home to express army's support for him; photo (M)

2000-08-09.title: McCaffrey Voices Confidence in Mexico's Anti-Drug Efforts
2000-08-09.abstract: Gen Barry R McCaffrey, visiting Mexico as United States drug policy director, lauds Pres-elect Vicente Fox's transition team; says he shares Fox's opposition to American drug-certification program but sees no chance that Congress will end it soon (M)

2000-08-10.title: Moscow Arrests Suspects but Can't Link Any to Fatal Bombing
2000-08-10.abstract: Russia detains number of suspects in terrorist bombing of Moscow underground pedestrian walkway; says none can so far be directly linked to blast; Pres Vladimir V Putin rebukes Mayor Yuri M Luzhkov for blaming Chechen rebels without proof; says Russia will complete military campaign in Chechnya and finish off terrorists; security forces scour subway stations and passageways for more bombs; reportedly find explosives at railway station (M)

2000-08-10.title: World Briefing
2000-08-10.abstract: MIDDLE EAST    EGYPT: DEFENDING PEACE EFFORTS -- In the first high-level response to Clinton administration critics, Osama el-Baz, an aide to President Hosni Mubarak, gave a spirited defense of the Egyptian role in advancing Arab-Israeli peace. ''It is utter nonsense to think we are urging the Palestinian negotiators to harden their positions,'' he said. ''We have every interest to encourage a closing of the gap between the parties.''    Susan Sachs (NYT)

2000-08-10.title: Iran Assembly Pushes on Women's Rights
2000-08-10.abstract: Iran's reformist Parliament shows its determination to forge ahead with social change and grant women additional rights by raising marriage age, changing law allowing fathers to marry off daughters as young as 9; will probably raise age to 15 or 16, although many reformers favor 18 (M)

2000-08-10.title: STUDY SAID TO FIND U.S. MISSILE SHIELD MIGHT INCITE CHINA
2000-08-10.abstract: Highly classified intelligence report reportedly warns that deploying American national missile defense could spur China to expand its nuclear arsenal tenfold and lead Russia to place multiple warheads on ballistic missiles; says Chinese expansion would prompt buildups by India and Pakistan; report underscores warnings by opponents that Clinton administration's proposal for limited defense system could lead to new arms race with Russia and, to lesser extent, in Asia; also underscores emerging threat to United States from missile proliferation (M)

2000-08-10.title: A North Korean Shift On Opposing U.S. Troops?
2000-08-10.abstract: SEOUL—  The North Korean leader Kim Jong Il, apparently signaling a shift in the North's long-standing insistence that U.S. troops leave South Korea, has opened the way to having at least some of them stay here, analysts said Wednesday.

2000-08-11.title: Kosovo Polje Journal; His Serbian Flock Scattered, the Priest Reflects
2000-08-11.abstract: Rev Radivoje Panic, having watched as Albanians fled from Kosovo and then as Serbs were driven from their homes, now serves at last remaining Orthodox church in town of Kosovo Polje, the Church of St Nicholas; few Serbs come to church, which is surrounded by barbed wire and patrolled by troops, mostly the old and those who want to light candles for the dead; Panic comments; map; photo (Kosovo Polje Journal) (M)

2000-08-11.title: World Briefing
2000-08-11.abstract:
EUROPE

NORTHERN IRELAND: NO OBJECTIONS TO A PARADE
Residents of the main Catholic neighborhood of Londonderry have dropped objections to a parade by 30,000 Protestants through the city on Saturday after rare direct talks between the rival communities produced agreements on limiting routes and reducing the police presence. Under an agreement hailed as a model for settling disputes during the so-called summer marching season, the Bogside Residents Association will not protest the annual parade by the Apprentice Boys organization celebrating the relief of the city in 1689 from the siege by the forces of the Catholic King James II.   Warren Hoge (NYT)

2000-08-11.title: Fear Spreads as Spanish and Basque Blood Flows
2000-08-11.abstract: Basque violence has exploded since last December, when separatists, angry at what they saw as lack of progress in negotiations with Spanish government, ended a 14-month truce; since then ETA has taken responisibility for widening wave of death and destruction; Spaniards are angry over renewed violence; vigils have been held across the country, and more than half a people marched recently in Madrid to protest ETA's resort to more assassinations and car bombings; scene in Durango described; map; photo (M)

2000-08-11.title: Russians Say Anti-Mine Group Aids Rebels
2000-08-11.abstract: Saad Eddin Ibrahim, Egyptian human rights activist who has been under investigation, is released on bail after being held in prison for 45 days without being charged; photo (M)

2000-08-11.title: Concorde Panel Says Metal Strip Probably Caused Tire to Burst
2000-08-11.abstract: French investigators looking into crash of Air France Concorde last month say that 16-inch piece of metal found on runway probably caused a tire to blow out, sending debris from it through fuel tanks and starting fire that erupted as plane took off (M)

2000-08-12.title: Canada Tries to Make Clear Its Diamonds Are Different
2000-08-12.abstract: Canadian officials, acting to protect their country's emerging diamond industry, are taking steps to ensure that their stones are never confused with 'blood diamonds' that help to finance guerrilla armies in Sierra Leone and Angola; Royal Canadian Mounted Police are developing technology to 'fingerprint' diamonds, identifying them by trace impurities that could be traced to known mines; map; photos (M)

2000-08-12.title: Czech Police and Army Get Ready for Protests at I.M.F.-World Bank Meeting
2000-08-12.abstract: Czech officials and private businesses are taking measures to guard against possibility of violent protests accompanying meeting of International Monetary Fund and World Bank in Sept; map; photo (M)

2000-08-12.title: U.S., Seeing Threat of Disease, Halts Trade in Argentine Beef
2000-08-12.abstract: US temporarily halts trade in Argentine beef because of concerns of possible outbreak of foot-and-mouth disease in Argentina (M)

2000-08-12.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-08-12.abstract: EUROPE    POLAND: WALESA CLEARED -- Lech Walesa, who fought Communist rule for two decades before its fall in 1989, was cleared of collaborating with the Communist-era secret police and will be allowed to run for the presidency, which he lost in 1995. He was forced to defend his reputation in a special screening court, which dismissed suggestions in old police files that he spied on fellow dissidents. Mr. Walesa, the founder of Solidarity, was scrutinized under a 1998 law that requires candidates to declare whether they cooperated with the Communist secret services. There is no penalty for admitting collaboration, but anyone found to have lied is banned from public office for 10 years.    (AP)

2000-08-12.title: A New Drive Gathers Steam To Avert Trial For Pinochet
2000-08-12.abstract: Gen Augusto Pinochet and his supporters have started campaign to gain support to help him avert trial on charges of kidnapping, murder and torture (M)

2000-08-13.title: Exiled Laotian Prince Seeks a New Role
2000-08-13.abstract: Prince Soulivong Savang, exiled grandson of last king of Laos, seeks to reassert his claim of leadership; prince escaped from Laos 20 years ago, fearing he would be killed by Communists who had taken control of country and who had killed his grandfather, father and other relatives; says he is more interested in restoring democracy than in future of monarchy; prince, who lives in Paris, has been following reports of student unrest and surreptitious return of Vietnamese military to Laos; he is also concerned about domination of Thai business on fragile economy of Laos; prince is in New York on visit to Laotioan exile communities in United States; photo (M)

2000-08-13.title: Koreans, Divided by War, Await Loved Ones' Return
2000-08-13.abstract: North and South Koreans involved in family reunions that will bring 100 North Koreans south and take 100 southerners north, are at forefront of effort to rebuild single Korean nation by first mending hearts broken long ago; exchange of family members separated by Korean War was given top priority at historic summit meeting in June between South Korean president, Kim Dae Jung, and North Korean leader, Kim Jong Il; meetings are regarded as strongest evidence to date that Kim Jong Il is committed to rapprochement; information is tightly controlled in North Korea; Kim is taking big gamble by allowing North Koreans even tiny glimpse into lives of their more affluent relatives in South Korea; photos; map (M)

2000-08-13.title: In a Warring Mexican Town, God's Will Is the Issue
2000-08-13.abstract: San Juan Chamula, in Chiapas State, is epicenter in Mexico's lingering conflict between Catholics and evangelical Christians; battles--often pitting Indians determined to preserve traditional customs against those who want change--have blazed for 30 years, dividing fathers from sons, turning violent, leaving hundreds dead and at least 30,000 displaced in Chiapas alone; that may be changing with new church, Prince of Peace Temple, which is being built with donations trickling in from evangelical groups across country; many evangelicals who left Chiapas to flee religious persecution have returned to help build church; Catholics seem to have begun grudging acceptance of growing evangelical congregation in their midst; photo; map (M)

2000-08-13.title: Taiwan Chief Drops Plan For Meeting In California
2000-08-13.abstract: Taiwan's new president, Chen Shui-bian, abruptly cancels informal reception with members of Congress after vigorous protests from China and expressions of concern by State Dept; reception had been organized by Rep Sam Gejdenson as chance for Chen to meet bipartisan group of lawmakers; Clinton administration officials say they do not favor Chen's meeting with lawmakers or other American officials in way that could be construed as official contacts; Gejdenson, a Democrat, issues curt statement about Chen being pressured by administration and State Dept to refrain from meeting members of Congress; cancellation appears to avoid potentially divisive diplomatic confrontation with China (M)

2000-08-13.title: U.N. Criticizes Lack of Help By Lebanon
2000-08-13.abstract: United Nations criticizes Lebanon for its very limited deployment of soldiers and police into south, 10 weeks after Israel ends its 22-year occupation of region; UN peacekeepers on ground hint that their mission, begun in 1978, could end when latest mandate expires in six months; Lebanon's refusal to have its forces go all the way to border is widely seen as move by Syria, which has virtually controlled Lebanon for decade, to maintain pressure on Israel to return Golan Heights; Lebanese government says it will deploy troops on border only when complete regional peace agreement is reached, including turnover of Golan Heights and return of Palestinian refugees (M)

2000-08-14.title: Fox Urges Opening of U.S.-Mexican Border to Build New Partnership
2000-08-14.abstract: Interview with Mexico's Pres-elect Vicente Fox, who says he will press US to open border to Mexican workers during upcoming visit to US and will begin dialogue on formation of North American common market; photo (M)

2000-08-14.title: 'The Grid' Is Next Wave of Computing, Labs Hope
2000-08-14.abstract: PARIS—  After the Web comes ... not the Matrix, but the Grid. And it's being brought to you by the same Swiss lab that created the World Wide Web.

2000-08-14.title: Washington Feels Time Is Short for Restarting the Mideast Talks
2000-08-14.abstract: White House is prepared to convene new summit between Israelis and Palestinians, but has concluded that effective deadline for final settlement during Clinton presidency is end of September; senior official says Pres Clinton is prepared to reconvene summit, but only if he is assured that Israelis and Palestinians are ready to settle all outstanding issues, including Jerusalem (M)

2000-08-14.title: Dongguan Journal; For Hong Kong Men, Mistresses on the Mainland
2000-08-14.abstract: China is seeking to put end to tradition of bigamous conduct, viewing wholesale adultery as social menace; is focusing efforts of 'concubine villages' that have sprouted in Guangdong province and elsewhere where hundreds of women live as mistresses or common-law wives of Hong Kong men, whose jobs require them to spend days or weeks at time on mainland; New Garden Village, one of most notorious concubine villages in Dongguan described; maps; photos (M)

2000-08-14.title: AIDS Cuts a Swath Through Africa's Teachers
2000-08-14.abstract: Large number of teachers are dying of AIDS in Ivory Coast and other parts of Africa, weakening continent's already troubled educational system; Ivory Coast, long economic crossroads as richest nation in French-speaking Africa, has highest rate of AIDS and HIV in region; nearly 11 percent of adult population is believed to carry virus, which killed 72,000 in 1999; government study found 641 educators died of AIDS between September 1996 and August 1998; ignorance regarding disease and condom use appears to be one of main causes of spread; difficulty of educating largely illiterate population is emphasized by fact that most teachers are not knowledgeable about disease either; spread of disease in neighboring countries and its high toll among educators discussed; maps; photo (M)

2000-08-15.title: Frantic Russian Effort To Rescue Crew of Sub
2000-08-15.abstract: Russian nuclear submarine Kursk plunges to seabed in Barents Sea during naval exercises, possibly after explosion on board, trapping more than 100 crew members 450 feet below surface; officials say vessel is badly damaged; are unsure how many of crew are alive or how long they can survive; Russian navy begins desperate rescue attempt; says odds of saving crew are slim; says submarine carried no nuclear weapons and that nuclear reactors were turned off, calming fears of possible radioactive contamination; Pres Clinton is briefed about accident and offers US help; American experts say vessel, unlike most Russian submarines, was probably not equipped with pressurized escape chambers that could have carried crew to surface; Russia's loss of at least four nuclear submarines from 1960's through 1989, and crippling of others by reactor malfunctions, fires or other problems recalled; photo; maps (M)

2000-08-15.title: Mixed Blood Makes Balkan Outcasts
2000-08-15.abstract: Ethnically mixed families in Kosovo, Serbia, are under threat both in Kosovo and rest of Serbia and are seeking refuge elsewhere; many have found refuge in Sarajevo, Bosnia, and elsewhere in Balkans; are seeking help from UN high commissioner for refugees to emigrate; photos; map (M)

2000-08-15.title: 2 More in Iran Are Detained In Crackdown On the Press
2000-08-15.abstract: Crackdown on reformers in Iran continues with detention of popular satirist, Ibrahim Nabavi, and newspaper columnist, Mohammad Qoochani; both are interrogated by hard-line court and detained; worked for banned daily (M)

2000-08-15.title: As Peace Plan Falters, Sri Lanka's War Has No End in Sight
2000-08-15.abstract: Gloom deepens in Sri Lanka as peace efforts of Prime Min Chandrika Kumaratunga, which would preserve Sri Lanka as unified nation while satisfying demands of Tamil minority for greater self-rule, collapse; many Sri Lankans hold out little hope that 17-year war with Tamil rebels will end soon; photo (M)

2000-08-15.title: In Summer, Italy Loves Sex Surveys; It's Just Talk
2000-08-15.abstract: Italy is awash in annual summertime sex surveys, which fill vacuum of political intrigue and soccer championships; while no country is exempt from weakness for news stories about sun and sexuality, Italy is perhaps one most adept at crafting bold headlines from slimmest scientific findings (M)

2000-08-16.title: With Safety in Doubt, British Ground Concordes
2000-08-16.abstract: British Airways grounds Concorde fleet after French and British investigators recommend withdrawal of supersonic plane's airworthiness certificate in aftermath of fatal crash in Paris; France says concern is about risks linked to aircraft's tires; Air France grounded its flights after July crash, first in Concorde's history; chronology of Concorde; photo; investigators believe 16-inch piece of metal on runway at de Gaulle Airport probably slashed tire as plane hurtled toward takeoff, creating debris that ruptured fuel tanks; emphasize that ruptured tire should not have such deadly consequences (M)

2000-08-16.title: London Journal; A Sex Scandal of the 60's, Doubly Scandalous Now
2000-08-16.abstract: London Journal recalls scandalous 1960's divorce of Margaret, Duchess of Argyll, which left behind tantalizing question of identity of one of her lovers, man photographed naked from neck down; new Channel Four television documentary concludes that two leading suspects, Duncan Sandys and Douglas Fairbanks Jr, were both involved; case recalled; photos; government's concern at time was for potential security breaches, since case coincided with Profumo sex scandal (M)

2000-08-16.title: Kashmir Endures Its Holiday of Contradictions
2000-08-16.abstract: Independence Day, as observed in Srinagar, unhappy summer capital of India's unhappiest state, is eerie kind of national holiday, with almost everyone staying indoors; Indian forces beef up security after Islamic separatist rebels threaten to disrupt celebrations; photo; prevailing sentiment in Kashmir Valley, place of legendary beauty and unremitting bloodshed, is wish for freedom and belief that India will never let go (M)

2000-08-16.title: As Peace Plan Falters, Sri Lanka´s War Has No End in Sight
2000-08-16.abstract: COLOMBO, Sri Lanka, Aug. 12 — This war-haunted nation's president, blinded in her right eye by a suicide bomber's attack, still talks of peace. But many ordinary Sri Lankans are more convinced than ever that no end to the country's 17-year conflict with the Tamil rebels is near.

2000-08-16.title: Demand Drives Oil Prices To Decade-High Levels
2000-08-16.abstract: NEW YORK—  Crude oil prices touched 10-year highs Tuesday thanks to a strengthening world economy that is increasing demand for energy.

2000-08-17.title: Like It or Not, North Koreans Tour a Mall in Seoul
2000-08-17.abstract: On their first full day in South Korea, 100 North Koreans get taste of capitalism when they are taken to giant shopping mall in Seoul as part of heavily orchestrated trip to visit relatives; with each Northerner officially allowed to see only five Southern relatives, some who did not make the cut seek quick meetings in mall and other public places; photos (M)

2000-08-17.title: Albright to Try to Shed Light on Missing Argentine Children
2000-08-17.abstract: Secretary of State Madeleine Albright, visiting Argentina on Latin American tour, pledges that she will work to declassify United States government documents that may shed light on kidnappings of hundreds of children from dissidents captured and imprisoned by Argentine military from 1976 to 1983 (M)

2000-08-17.title: Submarine's Namesake City Hurts As Minute After Minute Ticks Away
2000-08-17.abstract: Residents of Russian city of Kursk tensely follow attempts to rescue crewmembers from crippled submarine named after city; many local young men are aboard Kursk submarine; map; photo (M)

2000-08-17.title: German Party Poopers Do Their Best to Ruin a Celebration
2000-08-17.abstract: Plans for celebration of 10th anniversary of German unity on Oct 3 are turning into fiasco, revealing enduring divisions in the country and bitter feuding within Christian Democratic Party; Chancellor Helmut Kohl decides not to attend (M)

2000-08-17.title: Concorde Is Stripped of Certification to Fly
2000-08-17.abstract: British and French regulators withdraw Concorde's airworthiness certification, effectively ruling world's only commercial supersonic airliner unfit to fly until it can be protected against safety flaws behind last month's fatal crash near Paris (M)

2000-08-18.title: British Vasectomy Offer Brings French Outrage
2000-08-18.abstract: British group known as Marie Stopes International, for pioneering champion of birth control, offers vasectomies to Frenchmen, and Bernard Schnakenbourg becomes overnight celebrity by being first to cross Channel and have procedure performed; reaction in France, which is largely Roman Catholic, is largely one of outrage over what is perceived as English attempt to sterilize the French (M)

2000-08-18.title: Submarine´s Namesake City Hurts As Minute After Minute Ticks Away
2000-08-18.abstract: KURSK, Russia, Aug. 16 — Valentina Staroseltseva thought she was lucky, because her draft-age son could have been sent to fight in Chechnya, the worst nightmare of most Russian mothers.

2000-08-18.title: Survivors Tell of Submarine Horrors s
2000-08-18.abstract: Though no one knows just what agonies have befallen the men on the Russian submarine Kursk, other seamen who have been trapped beneath the sea describe how quickly the air can turn toxic and serious physical and emotional deterioration can set in.

2000-08-18.title: Russia Calls for Help From West As Hope for Sub Survivors Fades
2000-08-18.abstract: Russian naval forces failed again today to dock a rescue vehicle onto the sunken and disabled submarine Kursk as President Vladimir V. Putin ordered his military commanders to accept Western offers of assistance.

2000-08-18.title: Like It or Not, North Koreans Tour a Mall in Seoul
2000-08-18.abstract: On their first full day in South Korea after half a century, 100 North Koreans — in two controlled groups of 50 — experienced the capitalistic excess of a giant shopping mall today.

2000-08-19.title: Mogadishu Journal; A Peace-Starved City Weighs the Warlord Factor
2000-08-19.abstract: Many Somalis say warlords there do not wield as much power as they once did, but still enough to make trouble; courts and police units set up by Islamic groups have siphoned off their authority, as have business people, who not only hire their own militias but also provide jobs and money to people other than thugs; warlords' remaining power will be put to test if, as expected, the first Somali government sice 1991 is formed and actually tries to govern; photo (M)

2000-08-19.title: France and Britain Create Panel To Improve Safety of Concordes
2000-08-19.abstract: British and French aviation regulators form committee to examine ways to return the Concorde to service under conditions that would protect it against the flaws behind July 25 crash near Paris (S)

2000-08-19.title: As Peace Mission Deteriorates, U.N. Sends an Envoy to Congo
2000-08-19.abstract: Sec Gen Kofi Annan sends Gen Abdulsalami Abubakar of Nigeria to Congo to salvage what is beginning to look like a doomed United Nations peacekeeping mission (M)

2000-08-19.title: Kurds Warily Welcome Turkey's Changes in Human Rights Policy
2000-08-19.abstract: Turkey's Kurds welcome government's decision to sign two United Nations conventions dating to 1966 that guarantee the social and political rights of minorities; are looking to the government for progress on granting them new rights, including assurances that Kurdish can be spoken freely and that young people can be educated in Kurdish (M)

2000-08-19.title: Sydney-Bound Horses Face Toughest Animal Import Rules on Planet : Hooved Olympians Set for Longest Ride
2000-08-19.abstract: FRANKFURT—  At precisely 12:20 p.m. on Sunday, a Lufthansa 747 cargo freighter with 55 horses, 44 grooms and two veterinarians on board will taxi ever so gently away from a high-security warehouse at Frankfurt International Airport.

2000-08-20.title: U.N. Reform Group Assesses U.S. Candidates
2000-08-20.abstract: Independent United Nations watchdog organization, Campaign for United Nations Reform, says Vice Pres Al Gore would give more unqualified support to UN and be more willing to sign international treaties than Gov George W Bush; group had sent questionnaire to both men; Bush said he would never place American troops under UN command and would not pay United States debts to UN until its bureaucracy was reformed and American dues lowered; Gore said US should pay dues 'in full, on time and without condition' but he declined to endorse International Criminal Court, saying its treaty had significant flaws (M)

2000-08-20.title: Poland Spy Inquiry Said to Be a Failed Political Tactic
2000-08-20.abstract: Many Poles and Western diplomats believe that Marian Krzaklewski, Solidarity's current leader and presidential hopeful, was behind charges that Pres Aleksander Kwasniewski and former Pres Lech Walesa spied for Communist secret police; special court has cleared both men, and Kwasniewski, now that he has been cleared, is almost guaranteed re-election; photos (M)

2000-08-20.title: All 118 on Sub Probably Dead, Russians Assert
2000-08-20.abstract: Vice Adm Mikhail Motsak of Russian Navy says all 118 members of crew of wrecked submarine Kursk are now probably dead; says that in frantic initial hours, survivors trapped in sunken vessel had signaled their comrades on surface to send down air because their sealed compartments were filling with water; say some men may have tried what would have been suicidal exit through rear hatch, causing one of last dry compartments to flood; says most of sailors died in first minutes after still-unexplained explosion sent Kursk crashing to seabed; Norwegian divers with video equipment begin descent to assess damage to Kursk; Motsak, in statement broadcast on state television, says navy is investigating three possible causes of blast: collision with another vessel or World War II mine, or some unknown internal accident that set off explosion; photos; map (M)

2000-08-20.title: Colombia Pledges to Investigate Killing of 6 Children by Troops
2000-08-20.abstract: Colombian government promises full-scale investigation of army attack on elementary school hiking trip that left six children dead; top commanders of army initially blamed guerrillas for deaths, but various witnesses have come forward to say there were no rebels near shootings; episode comes at particularly embarrassing moment for Pres Andres Pastrana, who is preparing for Pres Clinton's six-hour visit on Aug 30 and for Secretary of State Madeleine K Albright to certify whether Colombia has met human rights conditions and is eligible to receive military component of new $1.3 billion aid program (M)

2000-08-20.title: Hassen Mall, 78, the First Nonwhite Jurist in South Africa
2000-08-20.abstract: Hassen Mall, Indian immigrant who became South Africa's first nonwhite judge, dies at age 78 (S)

2000-08-21.title: NORWEGIAN DIVERS OPEN SUB'S HATCH BUT FIND NO BODIES
2000-08-21.abstract: Team of Norwegian divers finally open damaged outer hatch of Russian submarine Kursk, lying on ocean bed more than 300 feet below surface in Barents Sea following accident, probably caused by onboard explosion; find rear escape chamber flooded but no bodies or any signs of life; rescue teams prepare plan to use ship's crane to rip hatch off vessel and to begin grim task of recovering bodies and lengthy and dangerous mission of salvaging submarines twin nuclear reactors; maps; photo (M)

2000-08-21.title: A Seaport Used to Pain Hurts Again
2000-08-21.abstract: Tragedy of sunken nuclear submarine Kursk, and apparent deaths of 188 sailors aboard, rattles residents of Murmansk, port that is home to large contingent of Russian navy personnel; photo (S)

2000-08-21.title: Corrections
2000-08-21.abstract: Correction of map accompanying August 18 article about research on chances of an earthquake at Hayward Fault in Northern California

2000-08-21.title: Sri Lankan Army Braces For Major Rebel Offensive
2000-08-21.abstract: JAFFNA, Sri Lanka—  The Tamil Tigers, the guerrillas fighting for an independent state in Sri Lanka, are poised for a major offensive intended to consolidate their hold on the Jaffna Peninsula, the commander of government forces on the peninsula warned over the weekend.

2000-08-21.title: NEWS SUMMARY
2000-08-21.abstract: INTERNATIONAL   A3-9    Rescuers Plan to Enter Sunken Russian Sub  Rescue teams were preparing a plan to use a ship's crane to rip a hatch off of the Russian submarine Kursk more than a week after it plunged to the bottom of the Barents Sea. They will then begin recovering the bodies of the 118 crew members and try to salvage the submarine's nuclear reactors.   A1    In a Russian navy town, the tragedy of the sunken submarine has rattled residents and brought a hush to the streets.   A6

2000-08-22.title: Russians Blame Cuts in Budget For Sub Fiasco
2000-08-22.abstract: Russia's defense minister, Marshal Igor D Sergeyev, defends military's failure to rescue crew of stricken nuclear submarine Kursk, blaming it on budgetary problems of past decade that have 'robbed and stripped' military; suggests military may have erred in rescue attempts, but insists no fundamental mistakes were made; says foreign assistance may have helped save crew; reiterates strongly held theory that Kursk may have collided with another submarine, which suffered severe damage but eventually managed to escape; his less than contrite remarks are seen as partial attempt to quell political storm incident has wreaked on government of Pres Vladimir V Putin; Norwegian divers end rescue operations after opening inner and outer hatches of vessel's escape chamber and finding no signs of life, and after failing in attempts to open rear escape hatch; say entire vessel was flooded; Kursk sank in 350 feet of water after unexplained but powerful explosions sundered hull during military exercises in Barents Sea on August 12; US and British governments categorically deny any of their submarines or surface vessels were involved; photos (M)

2000-08-22.title: China's Migrants Find Europe's Open Back Door: The Balkans
2000-08-22.abstract: Balkans have become latest gateway to Western Europe for tens of thousands of immigrants from China, South Asia and Middle Eastern countries like Iran and Iraq; Yugoslavia in particular, which has cultivated strong ties with China, has opened gates to thousands of Chinese intent on reaching Western Europe; its consulates in China freely grant tourist visas to Chinese, and legal passage into Yugoslavia eases way for illegal entry to rest of Europe; Bosnia has its own flow of illegal immigrants from Iran and Iraq and other countries with which Muslim-led government in Sarajevo has maintained strong ties, and Croatia is experiencing staggering increase in human traffic; influx underscores growing alarm among Western diplomats and migration officials that sophisticated and far-reaching network of human traffickers from Asia has switched attention from US and is looking to Europe; traffickers have found troubled and unruly countries of former Yugoslavia, with porous borders and lack of immigration laws and agreements, are ideal gateway to West; map; photos (M)

2000-08-22.title: Ex-Intelligence Agent Arrested in Britain on Return From Exile
2000-08-22.abstract: Former British security agent David Shayler, who has lived in self-imposed exile for past three years infuriating British government with series of disclosures about Britain's MI-5 and MI-6 ultra-secret security agencies, returns from France to face charges; is taken into custody and later freed on bail; is charged with violations of Official Secrets Act, sweeping law that outlaws unauthorized disclosure of any kind about workings of government; is ex-agent of MI-5 who quit in 1997, citing dissillusionment with agency; photo (M)

2000-08-22.title: U.N. READIES TEAM TO CHECK WEAPONS HELD BY THE IRAQIS
2000-08-22.abstract: UN assembles new team of arms inspectors to reenter Iraq within weeks, raising prospect of another confrontation with Pres Saddam Hussein over weapons programs; move comes two years after Iraq halted cooperation with previous group of inspectors, provoking diplomatic crisis and ongoing American and British air strikes; government has warned it will not cooperate with UN's new weapons commission, which Security Council ordered nine months ago in hopes of resolving objections to previous commission by Iraq, Russia and France; new team, with members from 19 countries, will be more accountable to UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan; will be headed by Hans Blix; members will work directly for UN, not for own countries as before; photo (M)

2000-08-22.title: Russia's Suspicion of Foreign Sub a Reminder of Cold War Chases
2000-08-22.abstract: Assertions by Russian officials that submarine Kursk sank in Barents Sea after collision, most likely with foreign vessel, echoes suspicions common during cold war, when on more than dozen occasions Russian and Western submarines banged into each other; Russians have long resented fact that many of collisions occurred in or near their waters and amounted to hit-and-run affairs, as American and British spy submarines quickly fled to safety; maps (M)

2000-08-23.title: Neo-Nazis Show No Remorse at Trial, and a German Widow Is Overcome
2000-08-23.abstract: German widow of African killed by neo-Nazis breaks down in court after facing three young men accused of murder in Dessau park; three show no remorse as trial opens; photos; map; slaying of Alberto Adriano has become symbol of virulence of rightist anti-immigrant sentiment in former East Germany, which has twice the unemployment of western Germany (M)

2000-08-24.title: Clinton Defends the Outlay Of $1.3 Billion to Colombia
2000-08-24.abstract: Pres Clinton defends his decision to sign a waiver freeing up $1.3 billion in anti-drug assistance for Colombia, saying that country is improving on human rights even though government of Andres Pastrana has yet to meet all the conditions imposed by Congress (M)

2000-08-24.title: U.N. Is Urged to Upgrade Peacekeeping Department
2000-08-24.abstract: Panel of international experts recommends that United Nations peacekeeping department be enlarged and professionalized, with intelligence-gathering functions that were essentially forbidden during the cold war; urges that more effort go into preventing situations from becoming crises and that peacekeeping officials resist allowing larger countries to exert too much influence over their day-to-day activities; recommendations are sure to displease American isolationists and some third world leaders who fear that a stronger, more independent organization would mean a new kind of colonialism (M)

2000-08-24.title: Newton's Alma Mater Is Passing the Hat to Purchase His Papers
2000-08-24.abstract: Cambridge University seeks donations to buy collection of letters, notebooks and autographed texts relating to work of Sir Isaac Newton, an alumnus, which is being offered for sale by Earl of Macclesfield; fears that collection will otherwise go abroad or be split among private collectors (M)

2000-08-24.title: World Briefing
2000-08-24.abstract: MIDDLE EAST    ISRAEL: SHAS CHIEF'S SENTENCE UPHELD -- The Supreme Court denied a request for a new hearing on a three-year jail term for fraud, bribery and breach of trust imposed on Aryeh Deri, former leader of the ultra-Orthodox Shas Party. It ordered Mr. Deri to begin his sentence on Sept. 3. Mr. Deri, a charismatic former interior minister of Moroccan descent who built Shas into a powerful legislative bloc, was convicted of taking bribes at the Interior Ministry.   John F. Burns (NYT)

2000-08-24.title: Cabinet Member's Death Puts Spotlight on India's Health System
2000-08-24.abstract: Death of P Rangarajan Kumaramangalam, a popular and lively leader of India's governing party, shakes country's political elite, many of whom believe that he got faulty medical care at one of New Delhi's most prestigious privately run hospitals; quality of health care for hundreds of millions of Indians is acknowledged to be miserable, but country's elected officials, and especially cabinet ministers like Kumaramangalam, are expected to get only the best (M)

2000-08-25.title: Neighbors Fear Fallout of Aid To Colombians
2000-08-25.abstract: Pres Clinton's upcoming visit to Colombia comes as country's neighbors fear increased US military aid will step up fighting that could push coca growing, drug trafficking, refugees and conflict across their borders; Colombian conflict has already led to guerilla incursions into Panama and Venezuela, rebel influence among Indian dissidents in Ecuador, and new Colombian plantings of coca and poppies in Peru; regional leaders fear impact of $1.3 billion in additional US aid over next two years to train and equip anti-narcotics brigade; Sec Madeleine Albright, visiting region, hears warnings from nervous Ecuadorean and Brazilian leaders; photo of Pres Gustavo Noboa of Ecuador and Pres Andres Pastrana of Colombia; map (M)

2000-08-25.title: Despite Soaring Prosperity, Poland Still Isn't Sure of Itself
2000-08-25.abstract: Poles feel discontent even though their country's new market economy is flourishing; largest, most successful transformation in central Europe has created two million small- and medium-sized private enterprises, such as wood-processing plant built from scratch by Urszula Malinowska and her husband in Biala Rawska; photos; map; Malinowska, who is likely to vote for reformed Communists, says changes have come with mixed blessings: small businesses do not get much help from government, tax laws are complex and contradictory, and being outside European Union allows Western trading partners to 'take advantage' of Poland; sourness is widely felt by Poles whose rapid progress is nonetheless falling behind their own expectations (M)

2000-08-25.title: China Arrests Evangelicals, Including 3 From the U.S.
2000-08-25.abstract: One hundred and thirty evangelical Christians, including three visiting Americans on religious mission, are arrested in Henan Province in central China during revival meeting; detained Chinese are followers of China Fangcheng Church, one of largest of many fervent Protestant groups evanglizing across country without government approval (M)

2000-08-25.title: Mexican President-Elect Warmly Greeted in Washington
2000-08-25.abstract: Vicente Fox Quesada, next president of Mexico, arrives in Washington to start laying out his vision of US-Mexican relations to present and future leaders of US: Pres Clinton congratulates Fox for winning fairest election in Mexico's history (S)

2000-08-25.title: Mexico Court Makes History By Siding With Congress
2000-08-25.abstract: Mexican Supreme Court orders Pres Ernesto Zedillo to turn over to Congress secret banking data relating to questionable financing of Zedillo's 1994 campaign; decision to uphold congressionanl authority marks clear declaration of independence from all-powerful executive branch (S)

2000-08-26.title: American Priest Dies in Kenya, And Authorities Suspect Murder
2000-08-26.abstract: Rev John Anthony Kaiser, American Roman Catholic priest, is murdered in Kenya; human rights groups suspect someone in government whom Kaiser had angered with frequent, and public, denunciations; he was found shot to death on rural roadside; photo (S)

2000-08-26.title: Clinton Adds Mideast Meeting
2000-08-26.abstract: Pres Clinton will add brief stop in Cairo, Egypt, to trip to Africa, to discuss Middle East peace talks with Pres Hosni Mubarak (S)

2000-08-26.title: Correction
2000-08-26.abstract: A New York Times article in editions of Aug. 17 about new information on a 1960s sex scandal in Britain that involved the Duchess of Argyll misattributed a lyric written for "You're the Top," the Cole Porter song inspired by the duchess. The lyric, with a reference to Mussolini, was written by P.G. Wodehouse for the 1935 London production of "Anything Goes." The lyricist was not Mr. Porter.

2000-08-26.title: China Agrees To Free Poet After Protests From Writers
2000-08-26.abstract: Huang Beiling, Boston-based Chinese poet arrested in Beijing on August 11 in connection with literary journal he edits, will be released and deported to US; is Chinese citizen with permanent-residence status in US; arrest drew protests from literary figures worldwide, as well as from US Embassy in Beijing; police confiscated hundreds of copies of Journal, Tendency, which is published in US, but gve no reason why they detained him; China's State Council Information Office says he was jailed because of illegal business activity (M)

2000-08-26.title: German Official Pessimistic About Far-Right Violence
2000-08-26.abstract: Foreign Min Joschka Fischer is pessimistic about eradication of rightist violence in eastern Germany; says it will take more than generation to overcome wounds of area that remains in 'twilight zone' between democracy and authoritarian tradition; says anti-immigrant violence that is chief focus of German political debate in past month is not confined to eastern Germany (M)

2000-08-27.title: U.N. Reports Afghan Curbs Hurt Populace
Lebanese, Sunk in Crisis, Deride Vote
2000-08-27.abstract: Lebanon is entering key parliamentary election marked by deepening economic crisis, widespread public cynicism and startlingly open criticism of vote rigging and Syrian domination; Rafik Hariri, multibillionaire builder who has raised hopes for rebirth of glittering Beirut of old, runs free-spending drive to become prime minister again; Pres Emile Lahoud's government attacks him with hours of special programming on state-run television; photo (M)

2000-08-27.title: Somalis Take Close Look At Election In the U.S.
2000-08-27.abstract: Somalis, despite mixed feelings about Americans, have strong affection for former Pres George Bush and back his son, Gov George W Bush, in United States presidential race; hope Gov Bush will reverse Pres Clinton's policy against American military involvement, although he has not signaled any plan for re-engagement; are growing optimistic that peace talks now under way in Djibouti may lead to return of government, but say any new government would need some funding (M)

2000-08-27.title: CLINTON IN NIGERIA TO SHOW SUPPORT FOR CIVILIAN RULE
2000-08-27.abstract: Pres Clinton visits Nigeria in bid to boost nation's transition to civilian rule; meets with Pres Olusegun Obasanjo; speaks to National Assembly; urges Obasanjo to press other oil producers to increase output and cut prices; Obasanjo pledges to seek lower, or at least more stable, prices at Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries summit meeting; Clinton plans to announce series of new aid packages for Nigeria; says he will urge European nations to reschedule their loans to Nigeria and eventually forgive debt if nation stays on reform path; map; photos (M)

2000-08-27.title: Shut-Out Nigerians Talk Of Leaders With Scorn

2000-08-28.title: Philippine Rebels Free 5 Foreign Hostages
2000-08-28.abstract: Five foreign hostages are released by Muslim separatist kidnappers in southern Philippines for reported $5 million ransom; releases are half-step toward ending four-month-long standoff on island of Jolo; four of freed are from original group of 21, kidnapped at nearby diving resort in Malaysia; fifth is French television reporter, taken hostage after trekking into jungle to film them on July 9; seven other Westerners remain hostage; map; photo (M)

2000-08-28.title: Poland Finds Itself the Border Cop of West Europe
2000-08-28.abstract: Poland's frontier with Belarus will soon become European Union's future eastern border with wild, untameable and unformed east, and burden of blocking illegal trade and immigration across it will fall on Poland, which is being asked by union to erect new, post-Communist barrier near Nieznany Bor as price of membership; maps; photo (M)

2000-08-28.title: Tense Election in Lebanon Mired in Bitter Power Plays
2000-08-28.abstract: Voting begins in Lebanon's two-stage parliamentary election, overshadowed by Syrian dominance and bitter struggle between current government and its critics; acrimonious campaigns in Mansuriya, Christian heartland of Mount Hermon between Michel Murr, Syrian supporter, and opposition slate headed by wealthy businessman and former ambassador Nassib Lahoud discussed; map (M)

2000-08-28.title: Lynden Pindling, 70, Who Led the Bahamas to Independence
2000-08-28.abstract: Sir Lynden O Pindling, former Bahamas prime minister, dies at age 70; photo (M)

2000-08-28.title: PRESIDENT URGES NIGERIA TO FIGHT TYRANNY OF AIDS
2000-08-28.abstract: Pres Clinton urges Nigerians to eliminate taboos associated with AIDS in Africa, and to confront 'tyranny' of disease with same resolve they showed during decades of dictatorship; offers American support for Nigeria's efforts to confront disease, but pledges no new assistance; comments at women's conference in Abuja are bold challenge to Africa, where stigma surrounding AIDS and inadequate public education about dangers have contributed to its spread; Clinton visits rural village of Ushafa with daughter Chelsea for glimpse of how vast majority of Nigerians live; they receive rousing welcome; he winds up two-day visit by encouraging Nigerian businessmen to work to expand economy beyond its heavy reliance on oil; map; photos (M)

2000-08-29.title: U.S. Spy Sub Said to Record Torpedo Blast Aboard Kursk
2000-08-29.abstract: Acoustical tapes made by US nuclear-powered attack submarine Memphis and other vessels contain strong evidence to support leading American theory that onboard explosion, probably misfiring of torpedo, caused sinking of Russian nuclear submarine Kursk in Barents sea with loss of 118 lives; American officials rule out collision with another submarine, as claimed by Russians; say rocket-propelled torpedo being loaded or launched as part of exercise misfired, with its engine and fuel exploding; refuse comment on whereabouts or mission of Memphis and another American submarine that were spying on largest Russian naval exercise in years, when disaster struck Kursk on morning of August 12; photo (M)

2000-08-29.title: Lebanese Opposition Registers Gains in Early Vote
2000-08-29.abstract: Opponents deal sharp blows to government of Pres Emile Lahoud and Prime Min Salim el-Hoss, in first round of parliamentary elections, apparently winning 35 of 63 seats in northern Lebanon (S)

2000-08-29.title: New Yorker Imprisoned in Peru Gets New Trial in Treason Case
2000-08-29.abstract: Peru's Supreme Military Justice Commission, top military court, revokes life sentence of Lori Berenson, New Yorker imprisoned since 1996 following conviction for treason; grants her new trial in civilian courts, but she must remain in prison; lawyer says trial will begin soon; human rights officials and Berenson family members voice dissatisfaction; say first trial was unfair and that there is no confidence that second will be any better; case has long bedeviled relations between Peru and US and new trial may represent gesture by Peru to soothe relations, especially following country's tarnished presidential election; Pres Alberto K Fujimori, who was re-elected in May after campaign marked by irregularities and charges of voter fraud, declines comment; Berenson was charged with helping rebels of Tupac Amaru Revolutionary Movement plan aborted attack on Peru's Congress; photos (M)

2000-08-29.title: U.S. Says Cuba Causes Misery With Its Hard Line on Emigres
2000-08-29.abstract: US State Department accuses Cuba today of causing widespread suffering by refusing to allow citizens to leave island even if they hold American visas; will bar Ricardo Alarcon, president of Cuban National Assembly, from attending meeting of international lawmakers at UN; denies move is in retaliation (S)

2000-08-29.title: A Victory of Sorts for Abortion Rights in a Mexican State
2000-08-29.abstract: Mexican abortion rights groups celebrate victory against resurgent efforts by conservative politicians and church leaders seeking to chip away at already limited rights to abortion after Gov Ramon Martin Huerta sends backs to state Congress for further study bill passed by Legislature that would have made state only one in Mexico to extend ban on abortions to cases of rape; map; photo (M)

2000-08-30.title: World Briefing
2000-08-30.abstract: EUROPE    RUSSIA: BRINGING UP SUB'S VICTIMS -- Russian and Norwegian divers will begin next month to remove the bodies of the 118 sailors who died in the sinking of the nuclear submarine Kursk. Deputy Prime Minister Ilya Klebanov said that the divers would cut six holes in the sub and that Russian divers would enter to retrieve the bodies. The sub will be raised next year, he said. Mr. Klebanov repeated that Russia believes that a collision with a foreign vessel caused the sinking, rejecting American reports that a torpedo exploded inside the sub.   Michael Wines (NYT)

2000-08-30.title: Factory Closings in China Arouse Workers to Fury
2000-08-30.abstract: TIANJIN, China, Aug. 29 — The brick-walled Meite Packaging factory compound is nearly deserted now, its managers and machines hastily transferred in the last couple of days to a special development zone 30 miles outside this industrial city.

2000-08-30.title: Clinton Meets Mubarak on Mideast Peace Effort
2000-08-30.abstract: Pres Clinton meets with Pres Hosni Mubarak at Cairo airport to build Arab support for Middle East peace; he will continue effort in separate talks with Yasir Arafat and Prime Min Ehud Barak, both of whom will be in New York for UN meeting; Clinton makes refueling stop in Egypt on way home from Africa; he will leave for South America after brief return to Washington, continuing barrage of diplomacy in waning months of his term (M)

2000-08-30.title: Brazil Begins to Take Role on the World Stage
2000-08-30.abstract: Increasingly confident and assertive Brazil is emerging as both US partner and rival in Latin America, steering own course on political and economic matters and stepping onto world stage as never before; photo (M)

2000-08-30.title: Clinton Defends Colombia Outlay
2000-08-30.abstract: CARTAGENA, Colombia, Aug. 30 — President Clinton made a one-day visit today to this secure oasis of Colombia to lend backing to the nation's counternarcotics program.

2000-08-31.title: American's Philippine Captors Known for Torture
2000-08-31.abstract: Muslim rebel gunmen who kidnapped American Jeffrey Schilling in southern Philippines belong to particularly brutal faction that has tortured and killed hostages in past; United States State Department rules out talks with rebels; says US would welcome mediation by nations like Libya, which has longstanding contacts with rebels (M)

2000-08-31.title: CLINTON DEFENDS COLOMBIA OUTLAY
2000-08-31.abstract: Pres Clinton, accompanied by Republican and Democratic lawmakers, visits Colombia to lend backing to nation's counternarcotics program; insists $1.3 billion in planned military aid is limited to drug fight, and will not involve United States in government's battle with rebels; says continuing aid will depend on government's breaking ties with paramilitary groups and adhering to strict human rights standards; rebels and protesters are active in advance of visit; Clinton tours port used by smugglers, with Pres Andres Pastrana acting as translator; photos; map (M)

2000-08-31.title: Train Derails in Paris Subway, Injuring 24
2000-08-31.abstract: Mysterious subway derailment injures 24 passengers in Paris; car that tipped over skids to halt just three feet from train on opposite track (S)

2000-08-31.title: Three Neo-Nazis Are Convicted of Murder of African Immigrant
2000-08-31.abstract: Judge Albrecht Hennig finds three young neo-Nazis guilty of murder in beating death of an African immigrant, Alberto Adriano, in Dessau, Germany; finds Adriano was killed because of his skin color; cites need to put end to long series of such attacks in former East Germany; sentences Enrico Hilprecht to life imprisonment; sentences two juveniles, Christian Richter and Frank Miethbauer, to nine years in detention; Chancellor Gerhard Schroder calls for mass movement against racism and urges police and courts to crack down on extremists (M)

2000-08-31.title: Israeli Court Upholds Scholar's Rights to Dead Sea Scrolls Work
2000-08-31.abstract: Israeli Supreme Court rules unauthorized early publication of Dr Elisha Qimron's reconstruction of important Dead Sea Scroll violated his copyright on work; rejects appeal by Biblical Archaelogy Society president, Hershel Shanks, of lower court ruling that he infringed Qimron's copyright by including reconstruction in his forward to collection of photographs of scroll fragments society published in 1991 to bring previously unpublished documents to light (M)
